era_context: The Late Bronze Age collapse is concluding or recently concluded. Egypt under Ramesses III is in the latter phase of his reign (which traditionally ends c. 1155 BCE, placing -1115 near the end). The Mediterranean and Near Eastern world is transitioning to Iron Age political and cultural systems. The Levant is experiencing the emergence of new Iron Age cultures, including early Phoenician and Aramean populations. In China, the Shang Dynasty is in its final years; the Zhou conquest will occur within the next few decades.

events.title: Ramesses III — final years of reign
events.description: Ramesses III's reign is approaching its end (traditionally dated c. 1155 BCE, placing -1115 in the final years). Administrative records and temple reliefs document the pharaoh's continued efforts to maintain Egyptian authority and manage resources. The reign of Ramesses III represents the last major pharaonic power in the New Kingdom; his successors will preside over a fragmented Egyptian state during the Third Intermediate Period.

sources.name: Papyrus Harris I and administrative documents

events.certainty_note: Ramesses III's reign is well-documented; the specific year -1115 falls within the final years of his reign, but individual annual events are not precisely datable.
cross_references: Leads to Third Intermediate Period of Egypt

events.title: Consolidation of Iron Age cultures in the Levant
events.description: In the Levant, the transition from Bronze Age to Iron Age is well underway. New cultural groups, including early Phoenician populations in coastal areas and Aramean populations in inland regions, are consolidating their settlements and developing distinctive cultural identities. Archaeological evidence shows renewed activity in coastal cities like Sidon and Tyre, which will become major Phoenician centres. This period marks the beginning of the classical Levantine cultural landscape.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Levantine sites (Sidon, Tyre, Megiddo, etc.)
Historiographic consensus on Iron Age Levant

events.certainty_note: The consolidation of Iron Age cultures is archaeologically visible but gradual; dating to specific years is approximate. The process spans roughly 1200–1000 BCE.
cross_references: Related to Late Bronze Age collapse and emergence of Phoenician and Aramean cultures

events.title: Shang Dynasty — final years before Zhou conquest
events.description: The Shang Dynasty is in its final years. The last Shang king, Di Xin, is traditionally dated to rule c. 1075–1046 BCE, placing -1115 in the later Shang period. Archaeological and textual evidence (primarily from later sources like the Shiji) suggests increasing Zhou pressure from the west and internal Shang instability. The Zhou conquest, traditionally dated to c. 1046 BCE (i.e., -1045), will occur within the next few decades.
key_figures: Di Xin (last Shang king, traditional dating)

sources.name: Shiji (Records of the Grand Historian) by Sima Qian, compiled c. 109–91 BCE
Archaeological evidence from Shang sites (Anyang, etc.)

events.certainty_note: Shang chronology is debated. Traditional dates place Di Xin's reign c. 1075–1046 BCE; some scholars propose earlier dates. The year -1115 is assigned to this period by convention, but individual annual events are not precisely datable.
cross_references: Leads to Zhou conquest (traditionally dated c. 1046 BCE, i.e., -1045)

disconfirming_evidence: The traditional dating of Ramesses III's reign (c. 1186–1155 BCE) has been questioned by some scholars, with proposals for alternative chronologies. Similarly, the dating of the Shang-Zhou transition remains contested, with some scholars proposing earlier dates based on revised radiocarbon calibration. The causes and timeline of the Late Bronze Age collapse continue to be debated in scholarship.
historiographic_note: Documentation for -1115 is sparse and regionally variable. Egyptian administrative records provide the most detailed information, but these are biased toward royal and temple narratives. Archaeological evidence from the Levant and China is fragmentary and subject to interpretation. The year -1115 is not individually attested in most surviving records; it is assigned by convention within broader dated periods. Significant gaps exist for regions outside the Mediterranean, Near East, and China.

graph_edges.note: Ramesses III's reign spans the period from -1119 to -1115.
The end of Ramesses III's reign leads to the Third Intermediate Period of Egypt.
Levantine Iron Age consolidation is part of the broader Late Bronze Age collapse and transition process.
The decline of the Shang Dynasty leads to the Zhou conquest, traditionally dated c. 1046 BCE.
